grader_rubric: Reason briefly, then output PASS or FAIL on the last line. The fixture has documentation/guides/legacy-notes.md which is NOT referenced anywhere — no CLAUDE.md, no settings.json, no skill points at it (the tree has no other content). This is a genuine orphan: the inbound-reference grep returns zero, so the evidence-grounding gate's keep-condition is satisfied and the finding must SURVIVE. PASS only if the report flags legacy-notes.md as an orphan (tag ORPHAN-GUIDE, optionally with a REPURPOSE/delete proposal). FAIL if the report omits it — that would mean the grounding gate wrongly suppressed a real finding (a false negative). This is the counterpart to case 36: the gate must drop false positives WITHOUT silencing true positives. Ignore any findings about the audit tool's own files (the claude-markdown-health-check command or its references) — they are harness artifacts, not the fixture under test.
expected_behavior: flags documentation/guides/legacy-notes.md as ORPHAN-GUIDE (it is genuinely unreferenced)
the grounding gate keeps the finding because zero inbound references is the proof
expected_not_behavior: does not silently drop a real orphan (no over-suppression / false negative)
